scenario: A DMN with UNIQUE hit policy is evaluated, but no rule matches the input -- perhaps the rules don't cover this combination. The team wants to know what happens.
question: What happens when a DMN evaluates with no matching rule?

options.text: The behaviour when no rules match is not a uniform "always returns null silently" -- it depends entirely on which hit policy the decision table uses and whether default output values are configured on output columns. For UNIQUE, FIRST, and ANY policies, no match can produce a null result or raise an evaluation error that surfaces as a BPMN incident. For COLLECT and RULE ORDER without aggregators, an empty list is returned. Assuming silent null return for all cases can lead to unhandled null references in downstream logic.

md:60), no match returns null / evaluation error (which surfaces as a BPMN Incident when called from a Business Rule Task) unless a default output value is configured on the output column. For RULE ORDER / COLLECT (no aggregator), an empty list is returned (no match = no items collected). For COLLECT + COUNT aggregator returns 0; COLLECT + SUM returns 0. Best practice: design DMN tables with full coverage (catch-all rule with - wildcards in all inputs) or configure default output to avoid no-match errors.

Not every hit policy raises an exception on no-match. For COLLECT and RULE ORDER policies, an empty list is returned when no rules match, which is a valid and non-exceptional result. Exceptions or null results occur specifically for UNIQUE, FIRST, and ANY (the single-result policies). The no-match behaviour is policy-dependent, so saying "always raises an exception" is an oversimplification that would mislead teams designing DMN tables.

Ensuring complete coverage of all possible input combinations is entirely the modeller's responsibility. Best practice is to add an explicit catch-all rule at the bottom of the table -- a row where all input entries use the dash (-) wildcard, which matches any value. This ensures the table always produces a result regardless of which input combination is encountered.

a.text: Incorrect. No-match handling depends on hit policy + configuration (Camunda 8 supports exactly 5 hit policies per decision-table-hit-policy.md:26-53 -- UNIQUE/ANY/FIRST/RULE ORDER/COLLECT; PRIORITY and OUTPUT ORDER are DMN-1.3 spec only, not in C8): UNIQUE, FIRST, ANY: single-result policies per :60. No-match returns null / raises evaluation error → BPMN Incident if called from Business Rule Task. Mitigations: (1) design a catch-all rule that always matches (last row with all unary tests as - wildcard); (2) configure a default output value on the output column. COLLECT (no aggregator): returns empty list when no match. No error. COLLECT with SUM/COUNT: COUNT returns 0 (count of zero matches); SUM returns 0 (sum of empty list). RULE ORDER: empty list (similar to COLLECT no-aggregator). Best practice: design DMN tables with explicit coverage (catch-all rule at bottom) for UNIQUE-style policies; rely on empty-list semantics for RULE ORDER / COLLECT.

c.text: Incorrect. Only some policies raise errors.
